ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has no specific term for xiao long bao or truffle pork soup dumplings; closest parent 'dumpling food product' (FOODON_03315091) used.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A0EJW 'Dumplings, steamed' is the closest defensible parent; no specific code exists for xiao long bao or truffle-filled variants.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT concept exists for xiao long bao or steamed soup dumplings.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard restaurant serving of 5 xiao long bao pieces, each weighing approximately 30 g, totalling 150 g.

nutrient_source.title: Xiaolongbao (steamed pork soup dumplings) — USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy / Foundation Foods composite, cross-referenced with HPB Singapore nutrient data for dim sum dumplings
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service; Health Promotion Board Singapore
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No exact USDA or HPB entry exists for truffle pork xiao long bao. Values are adapted from USDA FoodData Central entries for steamed pork dumplings (FDC ID 169937 and related dim sum entries) and HPB Singapore data for steamed pork dumplings, with a minor upward adjustment to fat_g to account for the black truffle component (truffles contribute negligible macronutrient change at typical garnish quantities of 2–5 g per serving). The truffle addition does not materially alter the macronutrient profile relative to standard steamed pork xiao long bao. Values represent per-100 g of the finished steamed product.

gi_source.title: Steamed wheat-flour dumplings — analogous GI estimate based on steamed dim sum dumplings and wheat-based steamed dough products
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for truffle pork xiao long bao. GI estimated at 48 (low) based on University of Sydney GI Database entries for steamed wheat-flour dim sum products and steamed dumplings, which cluster in the GI range 45–55. The wheat-flour wrapper is the primary glycemic driver; the pork and truffle filling does not materially raise GI. GL per serving calculated as (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (48 × 35.25) / 100 ≈ 16.9, where carbohydrate_g per 150 g serving = 23.5 × 1.5 = 35.25 g.

hypertension.key_factor: High sodium content (~420 mg per 100 g; ~630 mg per 5-piece serving)
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit consumption of xiao long bao due to the high sodium content from soy sauce and seasoned pork filling, with a 5-piece serving contributing approximately 630 mg sodium — over 25% of the recommended daily limit of 2000 mg.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Moderate glycemic load (~16.9 per serving) from refined wheat-flour wrapper
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes should consume xiao long bao in moderation, as the refined wheat-flour wrapper produces a moderate glycemic load of approximately 16.9 per 5-piece serving; pairing with non-starchy vegetables and limiting portion to 3–4 pieces can help manage postprandial glucose response.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Moderate saturated fat (~2.5 g per 100 g) and elevated sodium from seasoned pork filling
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should limit xiao long bao intake due to moderate saturated fat from the pork filling and high sodium from soy-based seasoning, both of which can adversely affect blood pressure and lipid profiles when consumed frequently.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Steamed Truffle and Pork Xiao Long Bao (松露小笼包) is a premium variant of the classic Shanghainese soup dumpling, xiao long bao, featuring a delicate hand-pleated wheat-flour skin encasing a seasoned pork and pork-gelatin filling garnished with aromatic sliced black truffle. The dumplings are steamed in bamboo baskets, which allows the gelatin in the filling to melt into a rich, savoury broth that floods the mouth upon biting. The skin is thin, slightly chewy, and translucent when properly cooked. The black truffle adds an earthy, umami-forward aroma that elevates the classic pork filling. This dish is a signature offering at Din Tai Fung, a globally recognised Taiwanese-origin restaurant chain celebrated for its precision-crafted xiao long bao. Nutritionally, a 5-piece serving (approximately 150 g) provides around 297 kcal, 14.7 g protein, 10.8 g fat, and 35.3 g carbohydrate. The sodium content is elevated (~630 mg per serving) due to soy sauce and seasoned pork, making it a concern for individuals managing hypertension. The glycemic load is moderate (~16.9 per serving) owing to the refined wheat-flour wrapper. Clinically, this dish is best consumed in moderation by individuals with hypertension, Type 2 Diabetes, or cardiovascular disease. It is not suitable for halal or vegetarian diets. The dish is a popular dim sum item in Singapore and is widely associated with celebratory and fine-dining contexts.

confidence_reason: No exact nutritional entry exists in any approved primary source for truffle pork xiao long bao. Values are adapted from USDA FoodData Central steamed pork dumpling entries and HPB Singapore dim sum data. The truffle component is present in small quantities and does not materially alter macronutrient values. GI is an analogous estimate from University of Sydney GI Database data for steamed wheat-flour dim sum products. Confidence is medium rather than low because the base food (steamed pork xiao long bao) is well-characterised in approved sources and the truffle addition is a minor garnish.
notes: 1. Nutrient values are adapted from USDA FoodData Central steamed pork dumpling data and HPB Singapore dim sum nutrient data; no exact approved-source entry exists for truffle pork xiao long bao. 2. Black truffle is present as a garnish/flavouring at approximately 2–5 g per serving; its macronutrient contribution is negligible and does not materially alter per-100 g values relative to standard steamed pork xiao long bao. 3. GI of 48 is an analogous estimate based on University of Sydney GI Database entries for steamed wheat-flour dim sum products; no direct GI measurement for xiao long bao was found in the database. 4. Serving size of 150 g (5 pieces × ~30 g each) is based on standard restaurant portion conventions for xiao long bao. 5. vitamin_a_mcg_rae is -1 as it is not reported in the adapted source data for steamed pork dumplings. 6. vitamin_b6_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the adapted source data. 7. vitamin_b12_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the adapted source data. 8. folate_mcg_dfe is -1 as it is not reported in the adapted source data. 9. vitamin_d_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the adapted source data. 10. vitamin_e_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the adapted source data. 11. selenium_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the adapted source data. 12. This dish contains pork and is not halal or suitable for vegetarian/vegan diets. 13. The dish is classified as a Singaporean-served Chinese dish; multilingual names are provided in English, Chinese (simplified and traditional), Malay, Tamil, and Hindi per the Singaporean cuisine multilingual rule.

Assigned GI of 48 (low) as analogous estimate from University of Sydney GI Database for steamed wheat-flour dim sum products; GL calculated at 16.9 per 150 g serving.
Documented all -1 sentinel values in notes with explicit reason for each.
